templeName: श्री समर्थ सद्गुरू धोंडूतात्या महाराज संस्थान,विराळ
allRightsReserved: या ॲपचे सर्व हक्क ॲपचे निर्माते
blessing: श्री समर्थ सद्गुरू धोंडूतात्या महाराजांचे जीवन चरित्र भाग १
blessingMessage:
**श्री समर्थ सद्गुरू धोंडूतात्या महाराजांचे जीवन चरित्र**
।। श्री गणेशाय नमः ।।

१. जन्म
श्री समर्थ सद्गुरू धोंडूतात्या महाराजांचा जन्म इ.स. १८३० (शके १७५२) मध्ये कंधार तालुक्यातील ‘लोहा’ या पवित्र गावी झाला. त्यांच्या वडिलांचे नाव अंबादास महाराज तर आईचे नाव सौ. गंगाबाई (सासरचे नाव सखुबाई) असे होते.

तात्या महाराज हे यजुर्वेदी ब्राह्मण असून त्यांची शाखा माध्यांजन व गोत्र भारद्वाज होते. पिताश्री जुना लोहा येथील महादेव मंदिराचे पुजारी म्हणून कार्यरत होते. त्यामुळे घराण्यात लहानपणापासूनच धार्मिकता, पूजाविधी व ईश्वरभक्ती यांचे दृढ संस्कार झाले होते.

संत तुकाराम महाराज म्हणतात :
“शुद्ध बीजा पोटी, फळे रसाळ गोमटी”

याप्रमाणेच श्री धोंडूतात्या महाराजांचा जन्म भक्तिमय परंपरेत झाला.

२. कुल परंपरा
श्री समर्थ धोंडूतात्या महाराजांचे वडील अंबादास महाराज पाठक व माता सौ. गंगाबाई यांच्या पोटी दोन पुत्र झाले.

राम महाराज
धोंडू महाराज (नैष्ठिक ब्रह्मचारी)

धोंडू महाराजांनी आयुष्यभर ब्रह्मचर्याचे पालन केले. त्यामुळे त्यांना अपत्य नव्हते. मात्र त्यांच्या भावंडांच्या परंपरेतून संतपरंपरा पुढे चालू राहिली.

वंशावळ (Genealogy)
अंबादास महाराज
│
├── राम महाराज
│   ├── बाळकृष्ण महाराज
│   │   └── श्रीराम महाराज
│   │       ├── किसन महाराज
│   │       ├── नागनाथ महाराज
│   │       ├── शंकर महाराज
│   │       └── दिलीप महाराज
│   └── गणपती महाराज
└── धोंडू महाराज (नैष्ठिक ब्रह्मचारी)

३. बालपण
बालपणी तात्यांची अंगकांती गौरवर्णीय होती. शांत व विनम्र स्वभावामुळे गावातील लोक त्यांना आपलेसे करून घेत. मात्र इतर मुलांप्रमाणे खेळात वेळ घालवणे त्यांना रुचत नसे.

वडील महादेव मंदिरात पूजेस जात असताना तात्या नेहमी सोबत जात. हरिकिर्तन व पुराण श्रवणात ते उत्साहाने सहभागी होत. ऐकलेली गोष्ट तंतोतंत सांगण्याची त्यांची विलक्षण स्मरणशक्ती होती.

संत ज्ञानेश्वर महाराज म्हणतात :
“दशेची वाट न पाहता । वयसेचिया गावा न येता ।
बालपणीच सर्वज्ञता । वरी तयाते ।।”

४. जीवनास दिशा मिळाली
लहानपणापासूनच धोंडू महाराज अभ्यास, साधना व एकांत चिंतनात रमू लागले. त्या काळी शालेय शिक्षणाची सुविधा नसली तरी पिताश्रींकडून संस्कृत व शास्त्रांचा व्यासंग झाला.

नामदेव महाराजांचे दर्शन
एकदा श्री नामदेव महाराज उंबरजकर लोहा येथे आले. सहा वर्षांचे धोंडू महाराज आईसोबत दर्शनास गेले. आईकडून नामदेव महाराजांची महती ऐकून त्यांच्या मनात साधनेची ओढ निर्माण झाली.

नामदेव महाराजांचा आशीर्वाद
धोंडू महाराजांना पाहून नामदेव महाराज म्हणाले –
“हा मुलगा भविष्यात कीर्तिमान होईल. याच्या हातून हजारो लोकांचे कल्याण होईल. वयाच्या आठव्या वर्षी याला समर्थ गुरुंची भेट होईल.”

या आशीर्वादाने त्यांच्या जीवनाला निश्चित दिशा मिळाली.

५. श्रीगुरुंची भेट
काळाच्या ओघात नवा लोहा (विठ्ठलवाडी) येथील विठोबाच्या देवळात काशीचे तपस्वी महात्मा श्री सखाराम महाराजांचे आगमन झाले. अंबादास महाराज व गंगाबाई आठ वर्षांच्या तात्यांना घेऊन दर्शनास गेले.

श्री एकनाथ महाराज म्हणतात :
“कोटी जन्माची पुण्यसंपत्ती ।
जरी गांठी असेल आइती ।
तै जोडे माझ्या संतांची संगती ।
सत्संगे भक्ती उल्हासे ।।”
chamatkar1Desc: एका श्राद्धदिनी ब्राह्मण वेळेत न पोहोचल्यामुळे तात्यांनी प्रार्थना केली की विधी पूर्ण होईपर्यंत सूर्यास्त होणार नाही. आश्चर्य म्हणजे विधी पूर्ण होईपर्यंत सूर्यास्त झाला नाही. ब्राह्मणभोजनानंतर त्यांनी नमस्कार करताच सूर्य मावळला.
chamatkar2Desc: एकदा तात्यांच्या पायाला सर्पदंश झाला. लोकांमध्ये घबराट असताना ते शांत राहिले व म्हणाले विष बाधा करणार नाही. स्नान करून गायत्री मंत्राचा जप केल्यावर त्यांच्या शरीराचा रंग पूर्ववत झाला. त्यांच्या आत्मबलाची प्रचीती सर्वांना आली.
chamatkar3Desc: कुनकी गावातील जन्मतःच पांगळा भक्त १९०४ साली दर्शनासाठी आला. तात्यांनी त्याच्या मस्तकावर हात ठेवून उठण्यास सांगितले. आश्चर्य म्हणजे तो मनुष्य स्वतःच्या पायावर उभा राहिला. त्या दिवसापासून तात्यांना स्वतः उभे राहता आले नाही.
chamatkar4Desc: डोंगरशेळकीमध्ये उन्हाळ्यात पाण्याची कमतरता होती. तात्यांनी गुप्त झऱ्याचे स्थान दाखवून खड्डा खोदण्यास सांगितले. मार्गदर्शनानुसार भरपूर पाणी मिळाले आणि गावाला पाण्याची स्थायी सोय झाली.
chamatkar5Desc: विराळमध्ये धोंडुतात्या महाराजांनी दत्त मंदिर उभारले जे आजही भक्तांसाठी उभे आहे. मंदिर स्थापनेपासूनच ते येथे दत्त जयंती मोठ्या भक्तिभावाने साजरी करत असत. दत्त जयंतीसाठी पंचक्रोशीतल्या लोकांना तात्या महाराजांचे आमंत्रण असायचे. या निमित्ताने भजन, प्रवचन आणि कीर्तनांचे आयोजन होत असे. अनेक गावांतील भजनी मंडळी, कीर्तनकार आणि भाविक भक्त विराळमध्ये येत असत. त्या दिवशी विराळ गावात पंढरपूर यात्रेसारखे वातावरण निर्माण होत असे. भक्त हरी आणि दत्ताचा गजर करत विराळमध्ये दाखल होत होते. तात्या महाराजांनी आपल्या भक्तांना तुप-पोळीच्या महाप्रसादाची व्यवस्था करण्यास सांगितले होते. दत्त जयंतीचा सोहळा संपल्यानंतर मारुती मंदिराजवळच्या मैदानात पंक्ती बसू लागल्या. भक्तांची गर्दी वाढत होती आणि महाप्रसाद वाढणे सतत सुरू होते. तेवढ्यात एक भक्त धावत येऊन महाराजांना म्हणाला की तूप संपले आहे. अजून अनेक भक्त प्रसादासाठी येत असल्याचे त्याने सांगितले. महाराजांनी शांतपणे काही भक्तांना घागरी घेऊन चलम्यावर जाण्यास सांगितले. त्यानंतर तात्या महाराज पाट्यावर बसून ध्यानस्थ झाले. भक्तांनी घागरी पाण्यात बुडवताच चमत्कार पाहा त्या तुपाने भरल्या आणि त्या तुपाने महाप्रसाद वाढला. आजही विराळमधील तो चलमा या चमत्काराची साक्ष देत उभा आहे.
chamatkar6Title: आणि... मुर्तीकार पंधरादिवस आगोदरमुर्ती घेवून आला
chamatkar6Desc: १९९१ साली विराळ येथे श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या मूर्तीची प्रतिष्ठापना करण्याचे ठरविण्यात आले. त्या निमित्ताने गावात मोठा महायज्ञ आयोजित करण्यात येणार होता. मूर्ती बनविण्यासाठी विराळ येथील काही मंडळी जयपूरला जाऊन मूर्तीकाराला काम दिले. दरम्यान विराळसह कुणकी, जिरगा, धामणगाव, वांजरवाडा, कोळनूर, डोंगरशेळकी व ढोरसांगवी येथील भक्त महायज्ञाच्या तयारीला लागले. महायज्ञाचा दिवस जवळ येत होता. मूर्तीकाराने मूर्ती महायज्ञाच्या दोन ते पाच दिवस आधी आणण्याचे मान्य केले होते. परंतु एके रात्री धोंडुतात्या महाराज मूर्तीकाराच्या स्वप्नात येऊन म्हणाले, “लवकर काम संपवा, मला गावाकडे जायचे आहे.” मूर्तीकाराने सुरुवातीला ते स्वप्न समजून दुर्लक्ष केले. पण महाराज पुन्हा पुन्हा त्याच्या स्वप्नात येऊ लागले. तेव्हा मूर्तीकाराला महाराजांच्या चमत्काराची जाणीव झाली. त्याने कोणालाही न सांगता मूर्ती बनवून १० ते १२ दिवस आधीच विराळकडे आणली. मुदतीपूर्वी मूर्ती विराळमध्ये आल्यावर गावातील लोक आश्चर्यचकित झाले. मूर्तीकाराने तात्या महाराजांच्या चमत्काराबद्दल सर्वांना सांगितले. मूर्ती कुणकी येथे पोहोचल्यावर गावकऱ्यांनी ती तेथेच थांबवून स्वागत केले. भजनी मंडळी भजन करत आणि सुवासिनी पाण्याचे घडे घेऊन मूर्ती आनंदाने गावात आणली. त्यानंतर विराळ येथे महायज्ञ करून धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या मूर्तीची प्राणप्रतिष्ठा करण्यात आली.
place1Desc: शतकानुशतके जुना ऐतिहासिक मंदिर परिसर, पारंपारिक वास्तुकला आणि आध्यात्मिक वारसा दर्शवितो. या परिसरात प्राचीन काळातील बांधकामाची सुंदर शैली आजही पाहायला मिळते. शांत वातावरण आणि भक्तिभावाने भरलेले हे स्थान भाविकांना अध्यात्मिक समाधान देते. येथे येणाऱ्या भक्तांना इतिहास आणि श्रद्धेची अनोखी अनुभूती मिळते. मंदिर परिसरात अनेक धार्मिक कार्यक्रम आणि उत्सवही साजरे केले जातात. त्यामुळे हे ठिकाण श्रद्धा, परंपरा आणि संस्कृती यांचे सुंदर संगम मानले जाते.
place2Desc: श्री धोंडूतात्या महाराजांची पवित्र समाधी, खोल आध्यात्मिक महत्त्व आणि ध्यानाचे ठिकाण. श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांनी संजीवन समाधी घेण्याचा विचार करून विराळ येथील मठाच्या प्रांगणात मध्यभागी विवराप्रमाणे छोटे भूयार बांधून समाधिस्थळ तयार केले व आत जाण्यासाठी एक छोटे द्वार ठेवले. त्यावर मंदिर बांधून सुमारे तीन फूट उंच व दीड फूट रुंदीची श्री दत्तात्रेयांची मूर्ती आणली. इ.स. १९१८ (शके १८४०) साली आचारसंपन्न वैदिक ब्राह्मणांकडून होम-हवन व विधीपूर्वक त्या मंदिरात श्री दत्तात्रेयांच्या मूर्तीची प्रतिष्ठापना करण्यात आली. जिवंत समाधी घेण्याचा संकल्प करूनच श्री तात्यांनी ही योजना आखली होती. आजही विराळ येथे त्यांची जिवंत समाधी असून तेथे नवचैतन्य अनुभवास येते. येथे येणारे भक्त शांततेत ध्यान करतात आणि आध्यात्मिक ऊर्जा अनुभवतात. हे स्थान श्रद्धाळूंसाठी प्रेरणादायी आणि पवित्र मानले जाते.
place3Desc: आपल्या समृद्ध सांस्कृतिक आणि धार्मिक परंपरांची कथा सांगणाऱ्या संरक्षित ऐतिहासिक संरचना. या स्मारकांमधून प्राचीन काळातील समाजजीवन आणि श्रद्धेची झलक दिसून येते. प्रत्येक वास्तूमध्ये इतिहासाची एक वेगळी कहाणी दडलेली आहे. स्थानिक लोक आणि भाविक या स्मारकांना अत्यंत जपून ठेवतात. या ठिकाणी येणाऱ्यांना आपल्या परंपरेबद्दल अभिमान आणि जाणीव निर्माण होते. त्यामुळे हे स्मारक सांस्कृतिक वारसा जपणारे महत्त्वाचे केंद्र बनले आहे.
place4Desc: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांनी स्थापन केलेल्या दत्तमंदीरातील दत्तमुर्ती. हे मंदिर भक्तांसाठी श्रद्धा आणि भक्तीचे प्रमुख केंद्र आहे. मंदिरातील वातावरण अत्यंत शांत आणि पवित्र असल्याने येथे ध्यान व प्रार्थना करण्यासाठी अनेक भक्त येतात. दत्तमुर्तीचे दर्शन घेताना भक्तांना अध्यात्मिक समाधान मिळते. मंदिरात विविध धार्मिक विधी आणि उत्सव मोठ्या भक्तिभावाने साजरे केले जातात. त्यामुळे हे मंदिर परिसरातील एक महत्त्वाचे धार्मिक स्थळ मानले जाते.
place5Desc: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या काळातील पिण्याच्या पाण्याचा हा पवित्र स्त्रोत आहे. आजही तो त्याच जागेवर स्थित असून त्यामध्ये मुबलक प्रमाणात पाणी उपलब्ध आहे. पूर्वी मठात येणाऱ्या संत, भक्त आणि गावकऱ्यांसाठी हा पाण्याचा मुख्य स्त्रोत होता. या ठिकाणाला ऐतिहासिक आणि आध्यात्मिक महत्त्व आहे. अनेक भाविक आजही या पाण्याला पवित्र मानून श्रद्धेने भेट देतात. त्यामुळे हा स्त्रोत केवळ पाण्याचा स्रोत नसून परंपरेची आठवण जपणारे ठिकाण आहे.
place6Desc: वर्षभर संपूर्ण प्रदेशातून भाविकांना आकर्षित करणारे एक महत्त्वाचे तीर्थक्षेत्र. येथे विविध धार्मिक कार्यक्रम, उत्सव आणि कीर्तनांचे आयोजन केले जाते. भक्त मोठ्या श्रद्धेने येथे येऊन दर्शन घेतात आणि आशीर्वाद प्राप्त करतात. या ठिकाणाचे वातावरण भक्ती, श्रद्धा आणि आध्यात्मिक ऊर्जेने भरलेले आहे. यात्रेकरूंना येथे येऊन मनःशांती आणि समाधान मिळते. त्यामुळे हे स्थान संपूर्ण परिसरातील एक पवित्र आणि प्रसिद्ध तीर्थक्षेत्र म्हणून ओळखले जाते.

bhag1.name: श्री समर्थ धोंडूतात्या महाराजांचे जीवन चरित्र भाग १

janm.title: 1. Birth
janm.desc: Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj was born in the year 1830 (Shaka 1752) in the sacred village of Loha in Kandhar taluka. His father was Ambadas Maharaj and his mother was Smt. Gangabai (also known as Sakhubai after marriage). Tatya Maharaj belonged to the Yajurvedi Brahmin tradition. Their Vedic branch was Madhyandin and their gotra was Bharadwaj. Ambadas Maharaj served as a priest at the ancient Mahadev temple in Old Loha. Therefore, from childhood itself Tatya Maharaj grew up in an environment filled with worship, religious rituals, and devotion to God. According to the teachings of saints, being born in such a pure and devotional family naturally awakened spiritual brilliance in Tatya Maharaj from a very young age. Their family had been devoted to spirituality for generations, and thus he was born into a deeply pious and cultured household.

kulParampara.title: 2. Family Lineage
kulParampara.desc: Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj was born to Ambadas Maharaj Pathak and Smt. Gangabai. They had two sons – Ram Maharaj and Dhondu Maharaj. Dhondu Maharaj observed lifelong strict celibacy, therefore he had no descendants. However, the spiritual lineage continued through Ram Maharaj.
kulParampara.genealogy: Ram Maharaj had two sons – Balkrishna Maharaj and Ganpati Maharaj. Balkrishna Maharaj had a son named Shriram Maharaj. Shriram Maharaj had four sons – Kisan Maharaj, Nagnath Maharaj, Shankar Maharaj, and Dilip Maharaj.
kulParampara.quote: “When virtuous sons and daughters are born in a noble family, even God feels joy.” – Saint Tukaram Maharaj
kulParampara.note: In accordance with this saying, Dhondutatya Maharaj, born in such a righteous family, became renowned as a lifelong celibate saint.

balpan.title: 3. Childhood at Viral
balpan.desc: During childhood, Tatya was very charming, fair-complexioned, and calm by nature. Because of his humble nature, everyone in the village loved him. Rather than playing games, he showed greater interest in religious activities. He regularly visited the Mahadev temple with his father. He loved listening to devotional discourses, kirtans, and Puranic stories. His memory was extraordinary; he could accurately narrate stories he had heard. This ability developed in him a strong interest in learning Sanskrit from an early age.
balpan.quote: “Without waiting for maturity, even in childhood wisdom sometimes appears fully.” – Saint Dnyaneshwar Maharaj
balpan.meaning: Some individuals display extraordinary wisdom at a young age — this was also visible in Tatya’s childhood.

jeevanDisha.title: 4. Direction in Life
jeevanDisha.desc: During that period, due to the Nizam rule, schools were not easily available in villages. However, since Tatya was born in a Brahmin family, he received education at home. From his father Ambadas Maharaj he learned Vedic knowledge and religious values.
jeevanDisha.namdevDarshan: Once Namdev Maharaj Umbarajkar visited Loha. Six-year-old Dhondu Maharaj went with his mother to seek his blessings. Seeing the radiant face of the child, Namdev Maharaj blessed him and predicted that the boy would accomplish great work in the future and illuminate the lives of many people.
jeevanDisha.blessing: He also said that at the age of eight, the child would meet a great Guru who would guide him and help him master all forms of knowledge.
jeevanDisha.effect: After receiving this blessing, Dhondu Maharaj began practicing regular exercise, Surya Namaskar, studies, and devotional discipline. Even as a child, he developed a liking for worship and meditation in solitude.
jeevanDisha.quote: “Spend time in contemplation, live in solitude, bathe in the sacred Ganga, worship God, and circumambulate the Tulsi plant.” – Saint Tukaram Maharaj

shriGuruBhet.title: 5. Meeting the Guru
shriGuruBhet.desc: As time passed, a saint named Sakharam Maharaj from Kashi arrived in New Loha. Eight-year-old Tatya went with his parents to seek his blessings. It is believed that meeting a saint happens only due to the accumulated merits of previous births. The true path of spiritual realization can be discovered only through the grace of saints.
shriGuruBhet.quote: “Only when one has accumulated immense merits from millions of births does one attain the company of saints; through their association devotion blossoms.” – Saint Eknath Maharaj

bhag2.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 2

shriGuruSakharam.title: 6. Guru Sakharam Maharaj
shriGuruSakharam.desc: Sakharam Maharaj belonged to the Vajaseneyi branch and Bharadwaj gotra. At a young age he embraced renunciation and began pilgrimages. Later in life he reached Pandharpur, where he realized that true spiritual knowledge cannot be attained without a Sadguru. Therefore he performed rigorous spiritual observances and fasting at the feet of Lord Pandurang, praying for a true Guru. After several days he received a divine vision instructing him to go to Kandhar and meet a saint named Hanumantaraya. Following this guidance, he received the Mahavakya initiation from his Guru and attained spiritual fulfillment.
shriGuruSakharam.lohaArrival: Later he settled in Loha, where a self-manifested idol of Lord Vitthal had appeared, making the place spiritually significant. Living in the Vitthal temple, he began teaching Vedic knowledge. His personality reflected intense renunciation. Once, after being criticized for wearing expensive garments, he renounced all luxurious clothing and adopted a simple lifestyle, maintaining lifelong celibacy. He also regularly undertook the Ashadhi and Kartiki pilgrimages to Pandharpur.
shriGuruSakharam.jataIncident: In his old age, the weight of his matted hair became difficult to bear. He prayed to Lord Pandurang, and by divine grace his heavy matted locks were symbolically offered at the Lord’s feet. Witnessing this miraculous event left people astonished. Such a powerful Guru blessing Dhondutatya Maharaj was indeed his great fortune.

moujibandhan.title: 7. Sacred Thread Ceremony
moujibandhan.desc: At the age of eight, in the presence of Sakharam Maharaj, Tatya underwent the sacred thread ceremony (Upanayan). By wearing the sacred thread he formally accepted the vow of Brahmacharya. His Guru adorned him with a Rudraksha mala and initiated him with sacred mantras. Under the Guru’s guidance he studied the Vedas for seven years and mastered many scriptures in a short period.

fatherDemise.title: 8. Father’s Demise
fatherDemise.desc: While Tatya was deeply engaged in studies and spiritual practice, his father passed away unexpectedly in 1845. At that time Tatya was only fifteen years old. This incident strengthened his sense of responsibility and determination.

viralgaon.title: 9. Arrival at Viral Village
viralgaon.desc: Gangabai’s brother, Bapurao Maharaj, brought Tatya and his mother to Viral village. As he had no children, he adopted Tatya. Living under his uncle’s guidance, Tatya cultivated discipline, humility, and a spirit of service. With the blessings of family members, his reputation continued to grow.

naishthikBrahmacharya.title: 10. Lifelong Celibacy
naishthikBrahmacharya.desc: When Tatya reached youth, discussions about his marriage began according to social customs. However, Tatya firmly declared that he would remain a lifelong celibate. Instead of worldly life, he chose the path of spiritual pursuit. Due to this firm resolve, he became widely known as a Naishthik Brahmachari.

tarunya.title: 11. Youth
tarunya.desc: During his youth he embraced discipline, spiritual practice, and devotion to God. Daily exercise, Surya Namaskar, yogic postures, and chanting of the Gayatri mantra became part of his routine. Through self-control and pure conduct he set an ideal example for society. His life was as pure as the sacred river Ganga, and through his actions he inspired spiritual awareness in people.

bhag3.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 3

bhakti.title: 12. Tatya's Devotion
bhakti.desc: The central focus of Shri Tatya’s life was devotion to God. Association with saints, daily worship, observing Ekadashi fasts, celebrating religious festivals, listening to spiritual discourses and kirtans, and singing the virtues of the Lord were the practices through which he nurtured his devotion. His faith was pure and unbroken. Just as a lamp illuminates the path in darkness, devotion illuminates the path of life. Because of this constant devotion, Tatya’s personality became radiant and spiritually powerful. As Saint Eknath Maharaj described devotion to God as the highest joy of life, similarly devotion itself was the true wealth in Tatya’s life. Because of this spiritual strength, he became worthy of the title 'Samarth'.

upasana.title: 13. Tatya’s Spiritual Practice
upasana.desc: Tatya observed lifelong celibacy and followed a strict daily discipline. Every day he practiced yogic postures, Surya Namaskar, and chanted the Gayatri Mantra. He never consumed food without performing Sandhyavandan in the morning, afternoon, and evening. He especially worshipped the Sun God. With the belief that the Sun is the witness of the entire universe and the source of all activities, he adopted the practice of Sun worship. Through this regular spiritual discipline, he developed extraordinary inner strength and divine radiance.

suryaGatiAvarodh.title: 14. Stopping the Movement of the Sun at Viral
suryaGatiAvarodh.desc: During a Shraddha ceremony, the Brahmins did not arrive on time, which created fear that the ritual would remain incomplete. According to tradition, the ceremony had to be completed before sunset. Tatya reassured his mother and said that the sun would not set until the ritual was completed. He drew a line near the Tulsi Vrindavan and prayed that the shadow should not cross it. Surprisingly, the sun did not set until the entire ritual and Brahmin meal were completed. After offering his prayers, the sun finally set. Everyone present was astonished, and the incident was considered a remarkable example of saintly spiritual power.

sarpdansh.title: 15. Snake Bite on Tatya’s Foot
sarpdansh.desc: Once Tatya was bitten by a snake. The people around him became frightened, but Tatya remained calm and fearless. He confidently said that the poison would not affect him. After taking a bath, he began chanting the Gayatri Mantra. Gradually his body returned to normal. This incident revealed his immense mental strength and unwavering faith in God. It also showed that saints remain free from worldly attachments such as greed and fear.

matechiKushi.title: 16. Blessed Lap of the Mother
matechiKushi.desc: Tatya’s fame and noble conduct spread everywhere. He was respectfully invited to religious events and ceremonies. Donations and offerings from devotees increased greatly. At the age of twenty-four, he began traveling on horseback to spread religious teachings. Seeing the virtues of her son, his mother Gangabai felt great joy. However, the law of the world is inevitable. In the year 1860 she passed away. At that time Tatya was thirty years old.

denagiVinayog.title: 17. Proper Use of Donations
denagiVinayog.desc: Some people questioned the large amount of offerings and donations received from devotees. Tatya explained that as a Vedic Brahmin he needed resources for religious duties such as rituals, pilgrimages, sacred baths during holy periods, and visiting saints. Funds were also used to organize annual Harinam Saptah and food donations on the death anniversary of his father. Following the teachings of Saint Tukaram, he used wealth only for righteous and charitable activities. His explanation satisfied everyone.

bhag4.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 4

vaidikAdhikari.title: 18. Authority in Vedic Rituals
vaidikAdhikari.desc: According to the Shastras, a Brahmin is ordained to perform six duties: Yajan, Yaajan, Adhyayan, Adhyapan, Daan, and Pratigraha. Shri Tatya followed these principles and his Swadharma with absolute devotion. Four qualities are considered essential to be an authority in Vedic rituals — the desire to perform the karma, the capability to complete it, scriptural knowledge, and an absence of opposition to the scriptures. Since all these qualities were fully present in Tatya, he became renowned as a proficient and respected authority in Vedic rituals.

pandhariYatra.title: 19. Arrival at the Holy Land of Pandhari
pandhariYatra.desc: Upon receiving the news of the passing of his Guru, Sakharam Maharaj, Tatya completed the necessary final rites. He built a Samadhi (memorial shrine) in memory of his Guru. Afterward, he went to Pandharpur, bathed in the Chandrabhaga river, and took the Darshan of Shri Vitthal. At that time, Tatya was fifty-three years old. His Guru's departure created a large void in his life, but he further strengthened his faith at his Guru's feet.

boriDaan.title: 20. Grain Service from Bori Village
boriDaan.desc: During a meeting at Umbraj, a moneylender from Bori village sought financial assistance for his daughters' weddings. Following the suggestion of Namdev Maharaj, Tatya assisted him. Out of gratitude, that moneylender took the responsibility of providing the necessary grain and expenses for the annual Harinam Saptah and Annadaan (food distribution) held on the death anniversary of Tatya's father. This sequence continued faithfully for many years. Later, by Tatya's command and the readiness of the villagers, this service was concluded.

durvaPooja.title: 21. Storing Durva for Worship
durvaPooja.desc: To ensure that Durva (sacred grass) required for daily worship was available throughout the year, Tatya brought Durva from the fields, made small bundles, and stored them in a cupboard. When a person asked in surprise, he replied that just as people store grain, I store materials for worship. This incident demonstrates his awareness and preparation regarding worship.

mathNirman.title: 22. Construction of the Math at Viral
mathNirman.desc: He resolved to build a Math (monastery) near the Maruti temple in Viral. After resolving land issues, he prepared a systematic plan. Construction began in the year 1894. Devotees cooperated wholeheartedly — some brought stones, while others provided wood. Within a few years, a large portion of the Math's construction was completed.

vishalVruksha.title: 23. The Massive Tree at Viral
vishalVruksha.desc: There was a massive tree on the outskirts of the village. Many superstitions surrounded it, and no one dared to cut it down. Since wood was required for the Math, the villagers requested to use that tree. Tatya gave them courage and stood at the site himself, giving the order to cut the tree. Despite frightening sounds, the tree was felled and its wood was used for the Math. Just as the Gita describes the tree of 'Samsara', spiritual strength is required to cut the tree of attachment and illusion. People began to believe that Tatya, through his spiritual power, had conquered such a tree of ignorance.

bhag5.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 5

jeevantSamadhi.title: 24. Resolve for Living Samadhi at Viral
jeevantSamadhi.desc: Shri Samarth Tatya had long ago firmly decided to take Sanjivan Samadhi (entering a meditative state for final departure while alive). In the courtyard of the Math at Viral, he constructed a small underground chamber to serve as the Samadhi site, with a small door for entry. He intended to install a three-foot-high and one-and-a-half-foot-wide idol of Shri Dattatreya above the Samadhi site. In 1918 (Shaka 1840), the idol was ceremoniously consecrated amidst Vedic rituals, fire sacrifices (Homa-Havan), and the chanting of mantras by Brahmins. All of this was done according to his prior planning.

suryopasakKiDattopasak.title: 25. Worshipper of the Sun or Dattatreya?
suryopasakKiDattopasak.desc: Tatya regularly performed Sun worship, offered Durva grass to the Sun, and performed Surya Namaskars. Nevertheless, he installed an idol of Shri Dattatreya in the Math instead of the Sun. This was due to the influence of his Guru, Sakharam Maharaj Kashikar. The Guru himself was a devotee of Lord Datta and had also installed a Datta idol. The Rudraksha necklace around Tatya's neck and the sandalwood and holy ash (Vibhuti) marks on his forehead were symbols of his devotion to Lord Datta. In his daily 'Panchayatan' worship, Dattatreya occupied the center, while the Sun, Ganesha, Vitthal, and the Shri Guru were seated around Him. Thus, it is clear that Tatya was a Dattopasak (worshipper of Datta).

tumhiItheMarnarNahi.title: 26. Lobhaji of Kolnur Village
tumhiItheMarnarNahi.desc: A loyal devotee named Lobhaji from Kolnur village used to serve Tatya. Once, while the Math construction was underway, he told Tatya, 'You are preparing for your Samadhi here, but your final resting place should not be here; you will have to go towards the south.' Later, in the course of time, Tatya took Samadhi at Dongarshelki, located south of Viral. Everyone experienced that the devotee's words had come true.

dagadSeva.title: 27. Fulfilling Desires through the Offering of Stones
dagadSeva.desc: A gentleman from Wakrad village in Kandhar taluka used to visit Tatya frequently for Darshan. Tatya told him, 'Do not come empty-handed; bring a stone with you.' At that time, the Math construction was in progress. Following the command, the man brought a stone every time. Seeing his devotion, Tatya was pleased. When the man expressed a desire for children, Tatya prescribed a vow of offering water to Lord Maruti daily and performing eleven circumambulations (Pradakshina). Within a short time, a son was born in his house. His wish was fulfilled by Tatya's blessings.

ashirwadMahima.title: 28. The Power of Tatya's Blessings
ashirwadMahima.desc: Devotees firmly believed that a word uttered by Tatya never went in vain. Due to his chanting (Japa), penance (Tapa), and concentration, his speech had acquired an extraordinary brilliance. Many experienced that the blessings he gave turned into reality. The belief that a Saint's word is truth was clearly evident through his life.

santDarshanMahima.title: 29. The Greatness of Seeking a Saint's Vision
santDarshanMahima.desc: Tatya was constantly immersed in singing the glories of God and spiritual practice. People came from far and wide for his Darshan. However, some acted out of hatred due to his progress. Once, a few people insulted the Saint, but one among them took Tatya's Darshan. Later, during a journey, the two who had insulted him were killed by a lightning strike, while the one who had taken Darshan remained safe. Following this incident, many stopped insulting Saints and embraced service with faith. This event conveys that those who harass Saints fall, while those who approach them with faith are liberated.

bhag6.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 6

bhaktasPaayDile.title: 30. Granting Legs to a Devotee in Kunki Village
bhaktasPaayDile.desc: In Kunki village, a short distance from Viral, there was a man who was lame from birth. Although he had legs, he could not walk. He had spent many years of his life dragging himself on his hands. Despite his suffering, he constantly chanted God's name. His faith in Shri Samarth Tatya was firm. In 1904, he came to Viral for Darshan with other devotees. Prostrating at Tatya's feet, he expressed his grief. Compassion arose in Tatya's heart. He placed his hand on the man's head and told him to stand up. To everyone's surprise, the man stood up on his own legs. All those present were stunned. From that day on, Tatya himself could not stand; the feeling grew among devotees that he had gifted his strength to the devotee.

menyatBasunPravas.title: 31. Shri Laxman Maharaj, Mahant of Umbraj Sansthan, at Viral
menyatBasunPravas.desc: Since the strength in his legs had diminished, it became impossible for Tatya to stand. This news spread everywhere. Shri Laxman Maharaj, the Mahant of Umbraj Sansthan, visited Viral. After a loving conversation, he insisted on sending a 'Mena' (palanquin) for Tatya. Tatya gifted his horses to him in return. Later, a palanquin was prepared and sent. From the age of seventy-four, Tatya began traveling in the palanquin.

avatarKarya.title: 32. The Purpose of His Incarnation
avatarKarya.desc: Devotees believed that Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj's incarnation was for the welfare of the people. He spent his life in Japa, meditation, worship, Sun worship, and spiritual endeavors. People received the message of the right path, self-restraint, and devotion through his conduct. Making society aware of sin and virtue and showing the proper way of life was the main objective of his life's work.

nimantran.title: 33. The Invitation from Dongarshelki
nimantran.desc: In the year 1926, a ceremony for the completion of a holy text was organized at Dongarshelki for the month of Shravan. The villagers decided to invite Shri Tatya as the chief guest. Tatya promised to come on the sixth day. On the scheduled day, the villagers came to Viral. Tatya inspired everyone with his radiant, elderly, yet divine personality. He prepared to depart in the palanquin.

vyasanTyag.title: 34. Message of De-addiction
vyasanTyag.desc: Before the palanquin was lifted, Tatya declared that those addicted to vices should not touch it. Hearing this, some stepped back in shame. Some repentantly took an oath to give up their addictions. Seeing their sincerity, Tatya gave them permission. In this way, Tatya gave society the message to stay away from addictions.

anandAnubhav.title: 35. The Joy of a Saint's Company
anandAnubhav.desc: The villagers lifted the palanquin while chanting the name of the Lord and moved southward towards Dongarshelki. Everyone experienced an extraordinary sense of joy in Tatya's company. Abhangs (devotional songs) were sung during the journey. All fatigue vanished. The mind was refreshed by the Saint's vision and company. That day, everyone experienced the true importance of 'Satsang' (the company of the holy).

bhag7.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 7

ratriAagman.title: 36. Arrival at Night
ratriAagman.desc: Shri Samarth Tatya was carried in the palanquin towards Dongarshelki. The people carrying the palanquin changed in shifts, singing Abhangs throughout the journey. It was the day of Ashwin Shuddha Ashtami. As they walked in the light of the half-moon in the evening, clouds gathered and hid the moonlight. Despite the darkness, the devotees felt no fatigue. Upon reaching near the village, the villagers cheered with joy; after the reading at the Mahadev temple was finished, the Aarti was performed, and everyone took Darshan of Tatya's feet. It was between 11 PM and 12 AM.

dhanyaDivas.title: 37. A Blessed Day and Fortunate Time
dhanyaDivas.desc: Tatya's arrival was a moment of great fortune for the village of Dongarshelki. To embrace a Saint's feet is to find peace of mind and the resolution of doubts into happiness and contentment. Shri Tukaram Maharaj says that meeting a Saint is very rare and requires past merits. The merits of the Dongarshelki villagers bore fruit, and they were blessed with Tatya's prolonged company.

prathamDivas.title: 38. The First Day of the Visit
prathamDivas.desc: Tatya first entered Dongarshelki village on Ashwin Shuddha Ashtami, Shaka 1848, between 11 PM and 12 AM. The next day was the holy festival of Dasara. Because of this auspicious date, Tatya's arrival was very significant. Just as Lord Krishna was born in Gokul at 12 midnight on Ashtami, Tatya's arrival also occurred on the night of Ashtami. The entire village was overjoyed by his arrival, and pure (Sattvic) emotions emerged in everyone's hearts.

snanPooja.title: 39. Rites of Bathing and Worship
snanPooja.desc: The next morning, some devotees began to bathe Tatya. Tatya touched the devotees, poured water over himself, and chanted the name of God. Tatya's disciples bathed him, dressed him in ritual silk (Sovala), and seated him in a sacred spot. Worship began, the Gayatri Mantra was chanted, and Surya Namaskar was performed using the 12 main names of the Sun. Sandalwood paste was applied, and holy water (Teertha) was distributed to the devotees. The villagers took Darshan with devotion and experienced Tatya's blessings.

annPrasad.title: 40. Great Devotion through Food
annPrasad.desc: After the completion of the reading, preparations for a community meal (Gaon-jevan) began. All devotees participated wholeheartedly in the work. When it seemed the food might not be enough, Tatya simply sprinkled a handful of rice. The food became sufficient, and the meal began. Even after the meal, food remained, which was distributed as 'Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj's Prasad'. Tatya's memory remained in everyone's hearts.

chamatkar.title: 41. The Reason for Miracles
chamatkar.desc: A miracle is never Tatya's permanent intent. He used divine powers when necessary to show the right path to atheists, the arrogant, or those focused only on the external world. Seeing miracles, atheists accept God, the arrogant become humble, and the outward-looking become introspective. Society walks the path of a proper life under the guidance of Saints. People experienced and described many of Tatya's miracles.

bhag8.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 8

navChaitanya.title: 42. New Consciousness Descends
navChaitanya.desc: On the day of Dasara, before the community meal, the final chapter of the 'Pandav Pratap' text was read in the Mahadev temple, the Aarti was performed, and food was offered (Naivedya) as the devotees prepared for worship. A disciple had prepared a special meal for Shri Tatya. In the evening, the Bhajan groups began singing with love, playing cymbals and the mridangam. Hearing the Bhajans, Tatya's joy increased, and he granted the request of the Bhajan groups. Every house was decorated, Rangolis were drawn, new clothes were worn, and festive delicacies were prepared; this joyful atmosphere filled Dongarshelki with a new consciousness.

mirvanuk.title: 43. Tatya's Grand Procession
mirvanuk.desc: Tatya's procession began through Dongarshelki village. Around 12 noon, musical instruments began to play, and Bhajan groups started singing Abhangs. Shri Tatya was seated in the palanquin, adorned with flower garlands, and the procession began with chants of 'Pundalika Varada Hari Vitthal'. Starting from the Mahadev temple, the procession moved through the main roads, completed a circuit of the town, and returned to the temple, where the Aarti was performed and the palanquin was stopped. Some devotees stayed near Tatya, telling him about the village.

dongarshelki.title: 44. This is Dongarshelki
dongarshelki.desc: Dongarshelki village is surrounded by hills and plateaus to the south. A small river flows near the village, which has a bend like the Chandrabhaga. In the center of the village is the tall fortress-like residence (Wada) of the Patils, surrounded by the houses of the villagers. The village gate is the main entrance; to the north is a temple of Maruti (the devotee of Rama who shows the path of devotion), and to the south is another temple. The layout of the village is very beautiful.

shivalay.title: 45. Shivalay (The Shiva Temple)
shivalay.desc: The Shiva temple is small but built in the Hemadpanthi style. From the outside, the stones look rough, but on the inside, there is beautiful linear stonework. There is a main entrance to the east, a square window to the south, a wall to the west where a small triangular niche for a lamp can be kept, and a horizontal stone shelf to the north. In the center of the temple is a north-facing Shivalinga, covered with a tin roof. Deities installed by ancestors are in the temple. The construction uses no small stones, only large stones.

dasaraSannidhya.title: 46. Dasara in the Presence of Shri Tatya
dasaraSannidhya.desc: Around 4 AM on Dasara morning, all the villagers gathered. They took Darshan by offering Tatya 'Apta' leaves and the grain sprouts from the Navratri 'Ghatasthapana'. Married women performed the 'Pancharti' and offered gold ornaments to Tatya to receive his blessings. Young and old alike celebrated the festival with joy by giving him the Dasara 'Tura'. Shri Tukaram says that such an opportunity to meet a Saint is a matter of great fortune.

punhaBhet.title: 47. Return to Viral
punhaBhet.desc: The next day was Ekadashi. The devotees respectfully sent Tatya back to Viral. The Bhajan groups accompanied him all the way to Viral, playing cymbals and mridangam. Devotees took his Darshan with Tulsi garlands, Gopi-chandan paste, and blessings. Some said, 'Maharaj, please stay in our village,' to which Tatya happily replied, 'I will visit again.' All the villagers returned, and Tatya arrived safely at Viral village.

bhag9.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 9

satkarm.title: 48. Shri Tatya's Good Deeds
satkarm.desc: Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj performed many meritorious deeds in his life. He built temples, installed idols, taught society the right path, steered people away from addictions, and organized Bhajans and Kirtans. In his Math, he immersed devotees in devotion by reading, listening to, and reflecting on the Bhagavat, Ramayana, and Mahabharata. He himself avoided pride, ego, and evil tendencies, keeping his mind and body pure through the chanting of the Gayatri Mantra, Surya Namaskar, and the ritual worship of Shri Dattatreya. He showed that remaining constantly cheerful and performing good deeds is essential for attaining Moksha (liberation).

dharmPrachar.title: 49. Propagation of Dharma
dharmPrachar.desc: Shri Tatya gave special importance to the propagation of Dharma. He dedicated his life to showing people the right path, explaining religious rituals, and emphasizing the importance of devotion.

sadhuSanga.title: 50. Company of the Holy
sadhuSanga.desc: Tatya always prioritized the company of the holy (Sadhusang). He spent his time interacting with Saints, listening to their experiences, and seeking their guidance.

guruBhakti.title: 51. Devotion to the Guru
guruBhakti.desc: Tatya's devotion to his Guru was incomparable. He spent his life serving his Guru Sakharam Maharaj, following his words, and receiving Guru-prasad.

shishyaShiksha.title: 52. Education of Disciples
shishyaShiksha.desc: Tatya provided religious education to many disciples. He taught them the Vedas, Upanishads, the Gayatri Mantra, and worship rituals. This ensured that religious traditions continued.

sanatanaDharma.title: 53. Sanatana Dharma
sanatanaDharma.desc: Tatya protected Sanatana Dharma. He increased the prestige of the religion by following the Puranas, Vedas, and Smritis.

vishwaBandhu.title: 54. Universal Brotherhood
vishwaBandhu.desc: Tatya gave the message of universal brotherhood. He encouraged loving all living beings, seeing everyone as equal, and maintaining a spirit of cooperation.

mokshaMarga.title: 55. The Path to Liberation
mokshaMarga.desc: Tatya's path was the path to Moksha. He taught that it is possible to attain the supreme state through the three paths of Devotion (Bhakti), Knowledge (Jnana), and Action (Karma).

bhag10.name: Life Biography of Shri Samarth Dhondutatya Maharaj Part 10

dongarPani.title: 56. Water for Dongarshelki
dongarPani.desc: There was a shortage of water in Dongarshelki during the summer. Tatya pointed out the location of a hidden spring and asked the villagers to dig a pit. Following his guidance, they found plenty of water. The villagers began using that water with joy, and the village gained a permanent water source.

upkar.title: 57. Shri Tatya's Favors
upkar.desc: Through his mercy and grace, Tatya increased the happiness of the villagers' lives. He removed their troubles and provided water. The debt of his favors can never be fully repaid in a human life; however, behaving with integrity while remaining aware of his grace is a human duty.

punyatithiSaptah.title: 58. A Week-long Festival for His Father's Anniversary
punyatithiSaptah.desc: On the occasion of his father's death anniversary, Tatya organized an uninterrupted week of Harinam (chanting) from Vaishakh Vadya Pratipada to Ashtami. Villagers and devotees from surrounding areas participated enthusiastically. Everyone performed all the tasks of the week with devotion and immersed themselves in Haribhajan.

darshanMahaprasad.title: 59. Tatya's Darshan and Mahaprasad
darshanMahaprasad.desc: On the seventh day (Saptami), Rangolis were drawn and decorations were made. Under Tatya's guidance, cooking preparations were made on a large scale. Thousands of devotees were overjoyed to receive his Darshan and get Prasad. Through the Saint's blessings, they benefited from a spirit of devotion and purity of heart.

event1.title: वार्षिक यात्रा २०२५
event1.description: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या वार्षिक सप्ताहानिमित्त गावात इंदुरीकर महाराजांचे कीर्तन आयोजित करण्यात आले. या कीर्तन कार्यक्रमाला गावातील तसेच परिसरातील अनेक भाविकांनी मोठ्या उत्साहाने उपस्थिती लावली. इंदुरीकर महाराजांनी आपल्या ओघवत्या वाणीने आणि प्रभावी शैलीत समाजप्रबोधनपर विचार मांडले. कीर्तनामध्ये भक्ती, विनोद आणि जीवनमूल्यांचा सुंदर संगम अनुभवायला मिळाला. उपस्थित भाविकांनी कीर्तनाचा मनापासून आनंद घेतला. या कार्यक्रमामुळे संपूर्ण परिसर भक्तीमय वातावरणाने दुमदुमून गेला. कीर्तनामुळे सर्वांच्या मनात भक्ती, संस्कार आणि सकारात्मक विचारांची प्रेरणा निर्माण झाली.

event2.title: रक्तदान शिबिर - वार्षिक यात्रा २०२५
event2.description: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या वार्षिक सप्ताहानिमित्त रक्तदान शिबिराचे आयोजन करण्यात आले. या पवित्र उपक्रमात गावातील तसेच परिसरातील अनेक युवक व नागरिकांनी उत्स्फूर्तपणे सहभाग घेतला. रक्तदान हे महादान मानले जात असल्याने सर्वांनी समाजसेवेच्या भावनेने रक्तदान केले. या शिबिरामुळे अनेक गरजू रुग्णांना जीवनदान मिळण्यास मदत होणार आहे. रक्तदात्यांना प्रमाणपत्र देऊन त्यांचा सन्मान करण्यात आला. या उपक्रमामुळे समाजात सेवा व सहकार्याची भावना अधिक दृढ झाली. रक्तदान शिबिरामुळे उपस्थितांमध्ये आनंद व समाधानाचे वातावरण निर्माण झाले.

event3.title: वार्षिक यात्रा २०२३
event3.description: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या वार्षिक सप्ताहात सांस्कृतिक कला महोत्सवाचे आयोजन करण्यात आले. या महोत्सवात गावातील तसेच बाहेरून आलेल्या कलाकारांनी उत्स्फूर्तपणे सहभाग नोंदवला. या कार्यक्रमात विविध प्रकारचे नृत्य सादर करण्यात आले. लहान मुलांपासून युवकांपर्यंत सर्वांनी आपल्या कलागुणांचे सुंदर सादरीकरण केले. विविध नृत्यप्रकारांमुळे कार्यक्रम अधिक रंगतदार झाला. उपस्थित प्रेक्षकांनी कलाकारांना भरभरून दाद दिली. या सांस्कृतिक महोत्सवामुळे वातावरणात आनंद व उत्साह निर्माण झाला.

event4.title: पालखी मिरवणूक सोहळा २०१८
event4.description: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या वार्षिक सप्ताहानिमित्त भव्य पालखी मिरवणूक काढण्यात आली. या मिरवणुकीत गावातील तसेच बाहेरून आलेल्या भक्तांनी मोठ्या उत्साहाने सहभाग घेतला. भजनी मंडळींच्या गजरात आणि टाळ-मृदुंगाच्या निनादात पालखी मिरवणूक निघाली. भक्तगण हरिनामाचा गजर करत पालखीच्या मागे चालत होते. सुवासिनींनी आरती करून पालखीचे स्वागत केले. संपूर्ण गाव भक्तीमय वातावरणाने दुमदुमून गेले होते. या सोहळ्यामुळे सर्व भक्तांमध्ये आनंद, श्रद्धा आणि उत्साहाचे वातावरण निर्माण झाले.

event5.title: वार्षिक यात्रा २०१७
event5.description: श्री समर्थ धोंडुतात्या महाराजांच्या वार्षिक सप्ताहानिमित्त सांस्कृतिक कला महोत्सवाचे आयोजन करण्यात आले. या महोत्सवात गावातील तसेच बाहेरून आलेल्या कलाकारांनी उत्साहाने सहभाग घेतला. कार्यक्रमात विविध प्रकारची नृत्ये सादर करण्यात आली. लहान मुलांपासून ते युवकांपर्यंत सर्वांनी आपले कलागुण उत्कृष्टरीत्या सादर केले. विविध नृत्यप्रकारांमुळे संपूर्ण कार्यक्रम अधिक आकर्षक व रंगतदार झाला. उपस्थित प्रेक्षकांनी कलाकारांना उत्स्फूर्तपणे दाद दिली. या सांस्कृतिक महोत्सवामुळे परिसरात आनंद व उत्साहाचे वातावरण निर्माण झाले.

facilitiesData.name: गंगामाता पाठक मंगलकार्यालय, विराळ
facilitiesData.description: आमचे मंगलकार्यालय तुमच्या सर्व विशेष प्रसंगांसाठी उत्कृष्ट सुविधा प्रदान करते. २४/७ पाणी आणि वीज पुरवठा, वधू-वर यांसाठी स्वतंत्र सुसज्ज खोल्या, स्वच्छ आधुनिक शौचालय, विस्तृत पार्किंग जागा आणि व्यावसायिक खानपान सेवा उपलब्ध. आम्ही तुमचे उत्सव संस्मरणीय आणि सुखकर बनवण्याची खात्री देतो.
